title: Parkio Daily Briefing for April 28, 2026
teaser: Walt Disney World rededicates the reimagined Wish Lounge during Disney Week of Wishes. Toy Story 5 products arrive ahead of June release.

bignews.title: Walt Disney World rededicates reimagined Wish Lounge at Magic Kingdom
bignews.body: The newly refreshed Wish Lounge reopened during Disney Week of Wishes with updated artwork and design inspired by wish-granting moments from Disney stories. The space serves as a retreat for Make-A-Wish families visiting the park. Six-year-old wish kid Paxton joined Disney executives and former wish recipient Abigail for the rededication ceremony.

bignews.title: Toy Story 5 merchandise now available ahead of June 19 premiere
bignews.body: New products featuring Woody, Buzz, Jessie, and newcomers like Lilypad and Smarty Pants are now on shelves. The merchandise line includes toys with tech features, collectibles, accessories, and books themed to the film's "toy meet tech" concept. The movie opens in theaters June 19.

topstories.title: Disney Week of Wishes celebrates Make-A-Wish partnership
topstories.body: Disney Experiences locations worldwide are celebrating the partnership with Make-A-Wish throughout this week. Since the first official wish 45 years ago at Disneyland, Disney has granted more than 175,000 wishes globally. A Disney wish is now granted every hour of every day.

topstories.title: Former wish kid now grants wishes as Walt Disney World cast member
topstories.body: Cast member Abigail, who visited the original Wish Lounge during her own Make-A-Wish trip over a decade ago, now works on the entertainment team helping families meet characters. She participated in the lounge rededication ceremony alongside wish kid Paxton and Disney leadership.

icymi.title: Walt Disney World grants more than 7,000 wishes annually
icymi.body: The resort welcomes thousands of Make-A-Wish families each year to experience character meet-and-greets, attractions, and special moments. The Wish Lounge provides a quiet space for families to rest during their visit.

spotlight.title: Planning your Disney visit?
spotlight.body: Check wait times and show schedules before you go. The parks are operating normally today with typical spring crowds. Arrive early for the most popular attractions and consider mobile ordering for quick-service meals to maximize your time.
